description: Discover why many different animals live at Big Break! Students will learn that all creatures have basic needs to live and grow, while they explore how some of the wetland residents find food and shelter in this watery habitat.

description: Learn about water in one of California's most significant watersheds – the Delta. Our 1,200 square-foot Delta map and targeted field/learning activities clarify watershed concepts, processes, and applications to students' lives.

description: Explore the science of the Delta and its powerful impact on everyday lives. Your class will create and test a hypothesis about the Delta ecosystem. Field work may include sampling water chemistry, cataloging wetland plants, investigating invertebrates, or more.

description: Evaluate the source of California's drinking water through scientific methods. Explore human impacts and discover the science of the Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta watershed.

description: This program introduces students to parts of the local indigenous culture. Students will learn about Native American People, their connection to the land and natural resources, and traditional skills.

description: There is no better way to explore and understand the wonder of the Delta than by kayak. FEE PROGRAM. Call 510-544-2753 for program details and pricing.
max_students: 12 people per rotation (max. 2 rotations per day)

description: Did you know that owls protect our homes and our food? Students will dissect owl pellets and discover how these wonderful birds provide pest control through their hunting habits. Students will also find out that we can help owls by protecting safe spaces like Big Break that they need to live and grow.
